title: Migrating from Uniface 10.3 Enterprise Edition
text: Migrating from Uniface 10.3 Enterprise Edition Uniface 10.4 adds and enlarges some fields in the Uniface Repository. Consequently, the IDE cannot work with a 10.3 Repository. You must therefore migrate Repository objects from Uniface 10.3 ot Uniface 10.4 . Prepare for Migration Review the changes made in Uniface 10. For more information, see What's New in Uniface 10.4.01 and Uniface 10.4.01: Repository Changes . Review the Platform Availability Matrix to ensure that your environment supports Uniface 10. Ensure that you use a Unicode-enabled database for the Uniface 10 Repository. Install Uniface 10.4 , if you have not already done so. It automatically creates a default Repository using an SQLite database. For more information, see Installing Uniface . If you want to use another supported database as your Repository, generate the SQL scripts to create the required Repository tables using the Create Table facility ( /gensql createtable ), then run these scripts in the appropriate DBMS. For more information, see Generate Scripts for Creating Repository Tables and /genSql . Migrate Repository Objects In your Uniface 10.3 installation, export your application definitions to XML files. You can use the Export Repository Objects form ( Main Menu ( ≡ ) > Export ) or the $ude ("export") function in your own export component. Import the export file or files into Uniface 10.4 . You can: In the Uniface IDE, choose Main Menu ( ≡ ) >Import XML . Run the Uniface IDE ( ide.exe ) with the /imp command line switch. For more information, see /imp . Create a component that uses $ude ("import") to import the files. For more information, see $ude ("import") . Uniface will import the data, and then migrate the definitions, and report warnings and errors about potential problems and failures. Compile the imported objects and test the application. For more information, see Compile and Test the Migrated Application . If you also migrated templates and pallets, you can choose reload the Uniface 10.4 templates which were overwritten by the import. Choose Main Menu ( ≡ ) > Load Templates and choose Load default templates . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
